rulings.Case Title: MORRIS IRVING VS. NANCY RODRIGUEZ ESPINOSA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Order To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Bring Case To Trial Within Five Years; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANTS NANCY ESPINOSA, MENELAO GALAN's Motion For Order To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Bring Case To Trial Within Five Years - Denied. The five-year period specified in Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310 was tolled as the action remained submitted to arbitration pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1141.17(b). rulings.Case Title: JERRY DUNCAN VS. BRUCE MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK MD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Liberally construed, Plaintiff's first and second causes of action include Defendant's alleged failure to obtain informed consent. There is a triable issue whether Defendant disclosed all pertinent information about the operations and obtained Plaintiff's informed consent. (Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal.3d 229, 240-41.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GWEN THOELE VS. SMITH & NEPHEW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount $5,500; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF GWEN THOELE'S MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount $5,500. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Based on Defendant's opposition, it appears that the only RFPD's at issue are Nos. 6, 8, 9, 15, 17, 24, 26, 28, 29, 32-36. The motion is granted as No. 6, 8, 9, 15, 17, 24, 26, 32-36. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses within 21 days of this Order. The motion is denied as to Nos. 28 and 29, as that information can be obtained more appropriately through interrogatories. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARY BOSQUE et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Of Reopen Discovery To Depose Plaintiff'S Treating Physician, Dr. Yoss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 7. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Motion to Reopen Discovery To Depose Plaintiff'S Treating Physician, Dr. Yoss. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. While the City did not conduct the deposition before the discovery cutoff, the moving papers establish excusable neglect, and the law favors trial on the merits. Moreover, Plaintiffs will incur little or no prejudice by the conducting of this two hour deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. BOILLOD VS. CHRISTINA M. WENDEL PHD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT CHRISTINA WENDEL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication denied on the negligence claim and granted on the breach of trust and fraud claims. There are triable issues whether defendant breached the standard of care and that breach caused plaintiff damages. Plaintiff does not present any evidence that defendant placing his own financial interests over the interests of plaintiff. Nor does Plaintiff identify an actionable misrepresentation. The foundation objections to the expert declarations are overruled. rulings.Case Title: MARY MURTAGH VS. JIAN LING GUAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative For Protective Order; Memo Of P&A; Exhibit "A"
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFF MARY MURTAGH'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff did not put at issue her medical and employment records by filing a lawsuit for wrongful eviction. The fact that damages may be measured by the number of years that Plaintiff will remain alive and stay in the apartment, does not establish justification for overriding established privileges, especially since the evidence requested by the subpoenas at issue would be tangential, and can be adduced and presented by Defendant by other methods. Moreover, evidence from Plaintiff's mediation brief is not admissible to support Defendant's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION, LOCAL 250A VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memo P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Demurrer To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award - Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. As currently pled, neither cause of action alleges a cognizable claim, although the second cause of action is not necessarily and clearly time-barred. As to the first cause of action, Petitioner is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege which mandatory "strictures" the Board failed to follow. As to the second cause of action, Petitioner is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts supporting a basis to vacate the arbitration award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F RYDER VS. SATEL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 16, CROSS DEFENDANT WILLIAM RYDER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT - Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third causes of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. The first and third claims are deficient as a matter of law and it does not appear likely that cross-complainants can cure the deficiencies. The first claim does not allege that Mr. William Ryder committed or threatened to commit an unlawful act. The third claim fails because the covenant of good faith does not extend to refraining of participating in the alleged conspiracy and there can be no liability for treating different defendants disparately. Cross-complainants are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege all of the elements of a fraud claim with particularity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CHEUNG VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 27, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT HARGEN'S INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Overruled. The second cause of action is adequately alleged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
